zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer於1460年出生在英國英格蘭戴文時，她的父親Lord Robert Spencer/ Sir Knight25歲，母親Eleanor Beaufort29歲。她於1490年與Sir Sir Richard de Spencer在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1501年8月16日在她的家鄉享年41歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊。

nameMeanings.name: Stuart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewart
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spenser
meanings: English: variant of   Spencer  .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: English:

occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer was born in 1460, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, Lord Robert Spencer/ Sir Knight, was 25 and her mother, Eleanor Beaufort, was 29. She married Sir Sir Richard de Spencer in 1490, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 16 August 1501, in her hometown, at the age of 41, and was buried in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer est née en 1460, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Lord Robert Spencer/ Sir Knight, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Beaufort, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Sir Richard de Spencer en 1490, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 août 1501, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterrée à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencerが1460年にイギリスイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父Lord Robert Spencer/ Sir Knightは25歳，母Eleanor Beaufortは29歳であった。1490年にイギリスイギリスデボンでSir Sir Richard de Spencerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1501年8月16日に故郷で41歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer(이)가 1460년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Robert Spencer/ Sir Knight(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Beaufort(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Sir Sir Richard de Spencer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1501년 8월 16일 그녀의 고향에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 버켈리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer родилась в 1460 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Lord Robert Spencer/ Sir Knight было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Beaufort, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Sir Richard de Spencer в 1490 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 16 августа 1501 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 41 год и была похоронена в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
